purpose: Continuous, read-only reconciliation verification substrate for persisted legitimacy lineage.
core_invariant: If lineage cannot be recursively reconciled, legitimacy integrity does not exist and verification returns NULL.

non_bypassability: scheduler is observability-only and never routes around /session -> /continuity -> /authority -> /compile -> /validate -> /execute -> /proof
verification cannot create authority, validation, execution, proof, continuity, PREO, or invocation rows
verification cannot reinterpret an invalid lineage as valid

checks: parent traversal to exactly one root

schema.timestamp: runtime emission timestamp only; not hash material

assumptions: remote lineage is evidence, not authority
canonical hash stability must survive runtime portability
trust boundary classification is explicit and bounded
rules: federated proof continuity must bind local proof hash to remote lineage reference without inferring remote legitimacy
cross-runtime replay claims must include runtime_id, decision_id, validated_object_hash, invocation_nonce, proof_id, and revocation_state
external authority lineage references can only narrow local acceptance; they cannot bypass local validation
revocation propagation semantics must classify remote revocation evidence as bounded trust input until locally reconciled

canonical_federation_verification_semant.foreign_lineage_handling: foreign lineage is bounded evidence and never inherited trust
canonical_federation_verification_semant.federated_replay_isolation: remote replay state is not reserved, consumed, released, or inferred locally
canonical_federation_verification_semant.local_validation_boundary: all portable or foreign evidence must still pass local validation before any local execution path can proceed

reconciliation_fate_matrix.corruption: proof without execution/validation/authority ancestry
continuity parent hash or parent pointer mismatch
invocation nonce differs between validation and execution
PREO authority_id or continuity_id mismatch
remote proof reference hash differs from local expected hash
same decision_id and validated_object_hash appear in competing proof/replay lineage
descendant remains active after ancestor revoked or expired
same registry state yields different traversal order
canonical hash re-derivation changes across passes
telemetry omits required reconciliation payload fields
foreign runtime lineage hash continuity breaks across trust-domain boundary
foreign ancestry claims a local root or mismatched parent lineage
same proof window cannot be ordered by canonical scheduler keys
report omits trace, anchors, deterministic id, timestamp, or drift classification
JCS canonical hash differs from portable envelope exact_object_hash
foreign replay tuple diverges from local decision/object/nonce lineage
canonical traversal sequence changes across equivalent inputs
portable reconciliation payload hash or drift class is corrupted
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation observability corruption
federated revocation exact-object or anchor corruption
federated revocation exact-object or anchor corruption
federated revocation exact-object or anchor corruption
federated revocation exact-object or anchor corruption
federated revocation exact-object or anchor corruption
portable identifier resolution cannot bind to canonical persisted row identifiers
composite traversal lookup key is offered as portable identity material
canonical persisted row identifier is absent during portable bundle emission
identical reconciliation lineage emits divergent checkpoint identity
checkpoint identity changes because observation timestamp changed

recursive_traversal_rules: start from exact persisted object under verification
follow explicit parent_continuity_id only; never infer ancestry
recompute canonical continuity hash at every depth
terminate only at exactly one root with no parent_continuity_id
detect cycles and over-depth traversal deterministically
preserve canonical traversal position in telemetry
replay_lineage_rules: validation, invocation, execution, and proof must share decision_id and validated_object_hash
invocation_nonce must remain identical from validation through execution
verification must not reserve, consume, or release replay state
duplicate lineage hashes and duplicate workflow_run_id evidence classify as replay drift
revocation_integrity_rules: revoked or expired continuity ancestors invalidate descendant legitimacy
authority, validation, and invocation descendants must reflect ancestor revocation
stale descendant ACTIVE/VALID/RESERVED states classify as revocation propagation drift
verification observes propagation only and never auto-heals stale rows
observability_constraints: drift rows cannot create, repair, or authorize legitimacy
payloads must be deterministic except emission timestamp
expected_lineage and observed_lineage must be canonicalized before hash evidence is computed
ambiguous drift classification returns NULL rather than best-effort classification

reconciliation_reporting_layer.timestamp_policy: timestamp is reported evidence only and excluded from deterministic reconciliation_id material

portability_layer.hashing: content-addressed lineage hashes over canonical payloads
portability_layer.exact_object_continuity: exact_object_hash must match the canonical payload being exchanged
portability_layer.drift_classification_semantics: portable drift classes remain enumerated and fail closed to NULL on ambiguity

federated_revocation_observability.validated_object_hash_anchor: canonical persisted validation_registry, aeo_registry, or proof_registry legitimacy lineage only
federated_revocation_observability.drift_classification_semantics: portable drift classes remain enumerated and fail closed to NULL on ambiguity
federated_revocation_observability.portable_identifier_policy: portable identifiers must resolve from canonical persisted registry row fields only; lookup_key and composite traversal anchors are not portable identity material
federated_revocation_observability.checkpoint_identity_policy: checkpoint_id hashes deterministic reconciliation state only; created_at is observational metadata excluded from identity
